description_long: This is a British Caledonian commercial jet airliner with a streamlined fuselage, swept-back low-mounted wings, and a T-tail configuration. The aircraft features two rear-mounted jet engines, each positioned on either side of the fuselage near the tail. The livery is predominantly white with a blue underbelly, a gold stripe running along the fuselage, and a blue tail fin adorned with a gold lion emblem. The cockpit is located at the forward end of the fuselage, with multiple passenger windows running along the sides. The landing gear is not visible in the images, suggesting it is likely retractable and currently stowed. The overall design is typical of short- to medium-haul passenger jets from the late 20th century.
description: A British Caledonian commercial jet airliner with a white and blue livery, swept low wings, and a T-tail, featuring two rear-mounted jet engines.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the fuselage, the British Caledonian branding, and the blue and gold tail fin with the lion emblem.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the T-tail, twin rear-mounted engines, and the full wingspan.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the same livery and features as view 0, with clear visibility of the passenger windows and engine placement.
description_view_3: Front view showing the rounded nose, cockpit windows, and the low-mounted swept wings extending outward.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing configuration with swept-back wings and no visible winglets; moderate dihedral angle.
attribute_tail_design: T-tail configuration with a single vertical stabilizer and high-mounted horizontal stabilizer.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Retractable tricycle landing gear (not visible, inferred from typical design).
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with multiple windows, typical of commercial airliners.
